summary: Completed comprehensive testing of PE Entity Mapping application. All 29 backend API tests passed (100%). Frontend testing verified fund selector, search, filters, entity tree visualization, and entity drawer functionality.
seed_data_creation: Database pre-seeded with 1756 entities and 1917 relations from CSV files
context_for_next_testing_agent: All backend APIs verified: /api/funds (52 funds), /api/entities (1756 entities with search/filter), /api/entities/filters (jurisdictions and entity_types), /api/entities/{id}, /api/funds/{id}/tree (Fund I has 71 nodes, 75 edges). Frontend verified: fund selector dropdown, entity search, jurisdiction/entity_type filters, entity list, tree visualization with React Flow, entity drawer from sidebar and tree node clicks, ownership percentages on edges, legend with Equity/General Partner/Top of Structure styling.

backend: GET /api/ - Root endpoint returns API message
GET /api/funds - Returns 52 funds with fund_id and fund_name
GET /api/entities - Paginated entity list with search/filter params
GET /api/entities/filters - Returns distinct jurisdictions and entity_types
GET /api/entities/{entity_id} - Returns entity detail with all fields
GET /api/funds/1/tree - Returns 71 nodes and 75 edges for Fund I
GET /api/funds/999999/tree - Returns empty tree for invalid fund
frontend: Fund selector dropdown loads all 52 funds
Selecting fund triggers tree render with correct node/edge counts
Search input filters entity list in sidebar
Jurisdiction and Entity Type filter dropdowns work
Clicking entity in sidebar opens entity detail drawer
Clicking node in tree opens entity detail drawer
Entity drawer shows all fields (name, jurisdiction, type, fund, asset, symbologies)
Tree displays ownership percentages on edge labels
Tree legend shows Equity (solid blue) and General Partner (dashed orange)
Top-of-structure nodes have black background styling
